# The `libml` package

## Overview

The `libml` package contains general functions necessary for the
analysis of biomarker data via statistical and machine learning
techniques, calculating KS distances, random forest, k-fold
cross-validation, classification performance, and ROC plotting.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Custom Installation

``` r

remotes::install_github("libml")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Usage

To load `libml` simply make a call to
[`library()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/library.html) as usual:

``` r

library(libml)
```

## Help summary of the package

``` r

library(help = libml)
```

## Notes about the package

- The `libml` package is easy to use.
- There is some cool stuff we can do.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### LICENSE

# Package index

## Univariate Tests

Generate a table of univariate tests.

- [`calc_univariate()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_univariate.md)
  : Create Table of Univariate Results
- [`mack_wolfe()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/mack_wolfe.md)
  : Mack-Wolfe Test

## Classifier performance

Utilities for evaluating (binary) classifier performance, including
confusion matrix tools.

- [`calc_confusion()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_confusion.md)
  [`print(`*`<confusion_matrix>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_confusion.md)
  [`summary(`*`<confusion_matrix>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_confusion.md)
  [`print(`*`<summary_confusion_matrix>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_confusion.md)
  : Calculate confusion matrix
- [`calc_brier()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_brier.md)
  : Calculate the Brier Score
- [`pull_stat()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/pull_stat.md)
  : Pull a Classification Performance Metric/Statistic
- [`get_max_cutoff()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get-cutoffs.md)
  [`get_spec_cutoff()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get-cutoffs.md)
  : Get Distance Cutoffs
- [`calc_auc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_auc.md)
  [`calc_emp_auc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_auc.md)
  [`calc_pepe_auc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_auc.md)
  [`calc_boot_auc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_auc.md)
  : Calculate Area Under Curve

## Training data

Tools for creating and manipulating training data objects of class
`tr_data`.

- [`create_train()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/create_train.md)
  [`is.tr_data()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/create_train.md)
  [`plot(`*`<tr_data>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/create_train.md)
  : Create a Training Data Object

## The fit\* family

Convenient wrappers for fitting various model types.

- [`fit_gbm()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/fit_gbm.md)
  : Fit a Generalized Boosted Regression Model
- [`fit_kknn()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/fit_kknn.md)
  : Fit Weighted k-Nearest Neighbor Classifier
- [`fit_logistic()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/fit_logistic.md)
  : Fit Multivariate Logistic Regression Model
- [`fit_nb()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/fit_nb.md)
  [`print(`*`<libml_nb>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/fit_nb.md)
  [`predict(`*`<libml_nb>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/fit_nb.md)
  [`plot(`*`<libml_nb>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/fit_nb.md)
  [`plot(`*`<naiveBayes>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/fit_nb.md)
  : Robustly Fit Naive Bayes Classifier
- [`stripLMC()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/stripLMC.md)
  : Strip Linear Model Components

## Model utilities

Predict, pull out components, manipulate, and interrogate model objects.

- [`calc_predictions(`*`<libml_nb>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/s3-calc_predictions.md)
  [`calc_predictions(`*`<naiveBayes>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/s3-calc_predictions.md)
  [`calc_predictions(`*`<randomForest>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/s3-calc_predictions.md)
  [`calc_predictions(`*`<gbm>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/s3-calc_predictions.md)
  [`calc_predictions(`*`<svm>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/s3-calc_predictions.md)
  [`calc_predictions(`*`<glm>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/s3-calc_predictions.md)
  [`calc_predictions(`*`<kknn>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/s3-calc_predictions.md)
  : Calculate Model Predictions
- [`get_max_cutoff()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get-cutoffs.md)
  [`get_spec_cutoff()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get-cutoffs.md)
  : Get Distance Cutoffs
- [`get_gini()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_gini.md)
  : Calculate Sorted Gini Importance
- [`get_model_coef(`*`<glm>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<lm>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<lda>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<libml_nb>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<naiveBayes>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<kknn>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<randomForest>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<gbm>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<glmnet>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<cv.glmnet>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<train>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  [`get_model_coef(`*`<svm>`*`)`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/get_model_coef-libml.md)
  : Get Coefficients of a Model
- [`select_features()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/select_features.md)
  : Select Model Features
- [`kfold_cv()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/kfold_cv.md)
  : Calculate k-Fold Cross-Validation
- [`reexports`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/reexports.md)
  [`calc_predictions`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/reexports.md)
  [`get_model_coef`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/reexports.md)
  [`get_model_features`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/reexports.md)
  : Objects exported from other packages

## Plotting utilities

Plot various model fitting related visualizations.

- [`plot_bayes_boundary()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/plot_bayes_boundary.md)
  : Plot a Naive Bayes Decision Boundary
- [`plot_boot_roc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/plot_boot_roc.md)
  : Plot a ROC with CI95
- [`plot_emp_roc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/plot_emp_roc.md)
  : Plot Empirical ROC Curve
- [`plot_log_odds()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/plot_log_odds.md)
  : Create a Log-Odds Plot
- [`plot_manhattan()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/plot_manhattan.md)
  : Create Manhattan Plot
- [`plot_uni_contrasts()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/plot_uni_contrasts.md)
  : Contrast 2 Univariate Tables
- [`barplot_auc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/barplot_auc.md)
  : Plot AUCs and Error Bars

## ROC related utilities

Tools for generating and evaluating Reciever Operaterator Criterion.

- [`calc_roc_fit()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_roc_fit.md)
  : Calculate ROC Curve Parameters
- [`create_roc_data()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/create_roc_data.md)
  [`filter_roc_data()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/create_roc_data.md)
  [`calc_roc_perpendicular()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/create_roc_data.md)
  [`calc_roc_corner()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/create_roc_data.md)
  : Create ROC Data Table
- [`geom_roc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/geom_roc.md)
  [`geom_rocfit()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/geom_roc.md)
  : Plot a ROC Curve
- [`plot_boot_roc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/plot_boot_roc.md)
  : Plot a ROC with CI95
- [`plot_emp_roc()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/plot_emp_roc.md)
  : Plot Empirical ROC Curve
- [`roc_xy()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/roc_xy.md)
  : ROC Curve Coordinates
- [`add_ss_box()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/add_ss_box.md)
  [`calc_joint_CI95()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/add_ss_box.md)
  : Add a Sensitivity/Specificity Box
- [`calc_ci_binom()`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/calc_ci_binom.md)
  : Calculate Binomial Confidence Interval

## Parameters

Common parameter definitions used by the package.

- [`params`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/params.md) :

  Common Parameters in libml

## Data

Package data objects.

- [`tr_iris`](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/reference/tr_iris.md)
  : Iris Data Set as Training Data

# Articles

### Getting Started

- [Introduction to
  libml](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/articles/libml.md):

### Nested Tibbles in Analyses

Short tutorial on how to use a list-column approach in simple univariate
analyses via simple t-test example.

- [Using Nested Tibbles for Univariate
  Analysis](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/articles/t-test-nested-analysis.md):

### Plotting ROC Curves

Short tutorial on how to plot ROC curves and generate AUCs using
multiple ggplot2-based plotting functions.

- [Plotting ROC
  Curves](https://stufield.github.io/libml/dev/articles/plotting-ROC-with-geom_roc.md):
